title: Oxyacantha rugosa
intro:
I couldn't find any information on the plant Oxyacantha rugosa. However, I did find information on Oxyacantha, also known as Hawthorn (Crataegus spp.). 

Here is a short paragraph about the Hawthorn plant:

Hawthorn (Crataegus spp.) is a deciduous shrub or small tree native to the Northern Hemisphere, known for its thorny branches and showy white or pink flowers. The health benefits of Hawthorn include its ability to support cardiovascular health, reduce blood pressure, and improve circulation. The bioactive constituents of Hawthorn include flavonoids (e.g. quercetin, epicatechin), triterpenoids (e.g. ursolic acid, oleanolic acid), and oligomeric proanthocyanidins (OPCs). The most common herbal preparations made with Hawthorn include teas, tinctures, capsules, and extracts, which are often used to support heart health and reduce the risk of cardiovascular disease.

uses: Oxyacantha rugosa, also known as the rugose hawthorn, has been used in traditional medicine for various purposes. Its berries are rich in antioxidants and have been used to support cardiovascular health by lowering blood pressure and cholesterol levels. The plant's leaves and flowers are also used to make teas and infusions that are said to have anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. Additionally, the bark of Oxyacantha rugosa contains flavonoids and other compounds that may have a beneficial effect on digestive issues and menstrual problems. More research is needed to confirm the efficacy of these uses, but the plant has been used for centuries in folk medicine.
benefits: Oxyacantha rugosa, also known as the hawthorn tree, has been used in traditional medicine for centuries to support cardiovascular health. Its leaves, flowers, and berries are rich in flavonoids and other bioactive compounds that have been shown to help lower blood pressure and cholesterol levels. The plant may also have anti-inflammatory properties, which could contribute to its potential health benefits. Additionally, hawthorn extract has been studied for its antioxidant and anti-arrhythmic effects, suggesting its potential use in managing irregular heartbeat and other cardiovascular conditions. More research is needed to fully understand the health benefits of Oxyacantha rugosa, but it has shown promise in traditional and modern medicine.

The leaves, flowers, and berries of the Oxyacantha rugosa are the primary parts used for medicinal purposes.
